input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-16T01:05:17.499Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads `Light` and `Dark` values and automatically applies the correct one when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent — use `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
// In your page constructor or setup method:
BackgroundColor = AppTheme(Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

var title = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
title.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"), Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
button.SetAppTheme(Button.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.White, Colors.Black);

// Helper
static Color AppTheme(Color light, Color dark)
{
    return Application.Current!.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark
        ? dark : light;
    // Note: SetAppTheme is reactive; the helper above is a
    // one-time read. Prefer SetAppTheme for live updates.
}
```

`SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, light, dark)` is the programmatic equivalent — it reacts to theme changes at runtime just like the XAML markup extension.

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reactivity** | Both approaches update automatically when the user toggles the OS theme — no manual subscription required. |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` (colors, images, strings, etc.). |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For larger apps, define `Color` resources with `AppThemeBinding` in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` to avoid repetition. |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a specific theme regardless of the system setting. |
